provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Geita District Council (Pro­duce cess) By-laws, 2003 and shall be deemed to have come into opera­tion on the first day of January, 2003.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These By-laws shall apply throughout of the area of jurisdiction of the Geita District Council.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-laws the following words and expressions shall have the meaning hereby assigned to them unless the context otherwise requires:— " buyer " means a person buying any produce at a market established for the sale and purchase of produce or any place within the jurisdiction of the Council, and includes a Cooperative Union, Cooperative Society, Company and parasitical organization; " council " means the Geita District Council; " produce " means any agricultural produce specified in the schedule to these By-laws;

provisions.title: Imposition of produce cess
provisions.content: There shall be levied and collected from every buyer a cess to be known as produce cess, on the produce specified in column one of the schedule to these By-laws which is sold and bought within the area of jurisdiction of the Council at the rate specified in column two of the schedule opposite to the specific produce .

provisions.title: Appointment of agent
provisions.content: The Council may appoint an agent or agents for collection of produces cess specified under By-laws 4 of these By-laws.

provisions.title: Variation of rate
provisions.content: The rate of the produce cess payable by the buyer shall be varied from time to time by the Council as it deems fit so to do.

provisions.title: When the cess becomes due
provisions.content: (1) The produce cess shall become due and payable by the buyer on the purchase of the produce specified in the schedule to these By-laws, by him. (2) The buyers hall immediately after buying the produce specified in the schedule to these By-laws pay the Council or its duly authorized agent, the produce cess due and payable by him.

provisions.title: Offence
provisions.content: Any buyer who refuses or fails to pay the produce cess due and payable by him under these By-laws is guilty of an offence and shall be liable upon conviction to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both fine and imprisonment.

provisions.title: Revoken of GN. No. 42 of 1984
provisions.content: The Geita District Council (Produce cess) By-laws, 1984 are hereby revoked.

provisions.title: Repeal not to waive liability
provisions.content: Not with standing the repeal of the By-laws under By-law 9 the produce cess payable under the repealed By-laws shall remain due until paid and nothing done in pursuance of the repealed By-laws shall be invalidated by reason of such repeal. Schedule Column one Column two Produce Cess Cotton 5% of Prurchase price Maize Tshs. 400/= per each sack Paddy Tshs. 400/= per each sack Beans Tshs. 400/= per each sack Cassava (udaga) Tshs. 300/= per each sack Groundnuts Tshs. 400/= per each sack Millet Tshs. 400/= per each sack Sorghum Tshs. 400/= per each sack Tomatoes Tshs. 200/= per each "tenga" (Small size) Tshs. 400/= per each "tenga" (Large size) Water melon Tshs. 30/= per each Pineapples Tshs. 10/= per each History of this document 04 April 2003 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 14 01 January 2003 Commenced Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
